nodes.label: Enterprise Platform Capability Model
nodes.description: Propose a practical capability model for enterprise platforms, including how platform capabilities should be grouped, how ownership should be assigned, and how RACI should be applied when products, departments, business domains, technology teams and control functions overlap.

source.subtitle: Capability groups, ownership model and RACI proposal

metadata.purpose: Propose a practical capability model for enterprise platforms, including how platform capabilities should be grouped, how ownership should be assigned, and how RACI should be applied when products, departments, business domains, technology teams and control functions overlap.
metadata.designStance: A platform is not a broad license, not a local system with many users, and not a technical department's default responsibility. A platform is a reusable, product-managed enterprise capability with explicit consumers, interfaces, controls, service levels, lifecycle, economics and decision rights.
metadata.scopeNote: Scope note This document is written for a large regulated retail and commercial bank with a Scandinavian operating context. It is intentionally organization-neutral and does not refer to any named bank. Vendor names are not used as ownership anchors; they should be mapped internally as implementation products within the capability groups.
metadata.coreRecommendation: Core recommendation Approve the capability groups in this document as the enterprise platform taxonomy. Use the taxonomy to classify current platforms, assign RACI, identify overlaps, and create platform charters for the highest-priority platform domains.

raciRoles.name: Enterprise governance forum
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Senior decision forum for platform designation, investment priority, material exceptions, and decommissioning.

raciRoles.name: Enterprise Architecture
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns taxonomy, target-state architecture, standards, capability boundaries, and cross-platform coherence.

raciRoles.name: Business capability owner
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns business outcome, critical service expectations, and process/product fit for business platforms.

raciRoles.name: Enterprise Platform Owner
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns platform mandate, roadmap, service catalogue, adoption, platform economics, and lifecycle.

raciRoles.name: Platform engineering and operations
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Builds, configures, operates, automates, supports, and improves the platform service.

raciRoles.name: Consuming product teams
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Consume the platform, build domain products on it, and own their applications, data, and operational use within guardrails.

raciRoles.name: Security and IAM
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns security policy, identity standards, security control interpretation, and security assurance.

raciRoles.name: Risk, compliance, privacy and legal
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns risk/control interpretation, regulatory alignment, privacy/legal requirements, and assurance coordination.

raciRoles.name: Data and AI governance
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns data governance, AI risk policy, metadata, lineage, data quality, and AI/model/agent governance.

raciRoles.name: Sourcing, vendor, finance and FinOps
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns sourcing, contract, vendor performance, license, cost transparency, chargeback/showback, and optimization.

raciRoles.name: Service management and operational resilience
raciRoles.role_in_platform_ownership: Owns ITSM process policy, resilience process, incident/change/problem model, recovery expectations, and evidence.

metadata.ownershipRule: Ownership rule Assign one accountable owner for the platform service, but do not confuse this with business outcome accountability or control-function accountability. For example, a customer-channel business owner may be accountable for the business outcome, while the platform owner is accountable for reusable channel platform services and security or data functions approve relevant controls.

ownershipByPlatformType.typical_examples: Customer channels, core banking, payments, lending, CRM, corporate platforms.
ownershipByPlatformType.consulted: EA, Security, Risk/Compliance, Data Office, consumers

ownershipByPlatformType.typical_examples: Hosting, runtime, mainframe, cloud landing zones, integration, developer platform, observability.
ownershipByPlatformType.consulted: EA, Security, Risk/Compliance, Service Management, consumers

ownershipByPlatformType.typical_examples: Identity, access, cryptography, GRC, operational-risk tooling, resilience tooling.
ownershipByPlatformType.accountable: Control owner such as Security, Risk, Compliance, Privacy or Operational Resilience

ownershipByPlatformType.typical_examples: Data lake/warehouse, BI, model/agent platforms, knowledge bases, data quality and lineage.
ownershipByPlatformType.accountable: Data/AI governance for policy; platform owner for service
ownershipByPlatformType.consulted: EA, Security, Risk/Compliance, Legal, consumer teams

ownershipByPlatformType.typical_examples: ITSM, service catalogue, configuration management, operational workflow automation.
ownershipByPlatformType.consulted: EA, Security, Risk/Compliance, Sourcing, all IT consumers

ownershipByPlatformType.typical_examples: Collaboration, office productivity, endpoint, information protection, employee portal.
ownershipByPlatformType.consulted: EA for boundary decisions, Security/Privacy, Data Office, business users

boundaryRules.implication: Classify the platform by the enterprise capability it standardizes, not by vendor product boundaries, license scope, or local implementation history.
Each major capability should have one primary platform home. Adjacent products may implement local or embedded features only under explicit scope rules.

boundaryRules.rule: Contract ownership is not platform ownership
boundaryRules.implication: Owning a vendor contract creates commercial responsibility, not automatic authority over every capability in the product suite.

boundaryRules.rule: Mandated reuse must be matched by product obligations
boundaryRules.implication: A platform can be mandatory only when it provides usable services, support, documentation, onboarding, evidence, and lifecycle commitments.

boundaryRules.rule: Exceptions are managed products of governance
boundaryRules.implication: Exceptions are time-bound decisions with a rationale, owner, risk acceptance, review date, and migration trigger.

boundaryRules.rule: Controls should be embedded in the platform
boundaryRules.implication: Security, compliance, observability, resilience, and evidence should be provided through self-service patterns rather than manual approval queues.

metadata.boundaryExample: Practical boundary example A workflow embedded inside a service management platform can be appropriate for IT operations processes. A workflow embedded inside a CRM platform can be appropriate for sales or servicing processes. A low-code workflow can be appropriate for local productivity automation. A cross-domain, critical, long-running, auditable business process should normally be assessed against the enterprise workflow/case platform unless a documented exception exists.

adoptionApproach.expected_output: List candidate platforms, mapped products, current owners, consumers, contracts, critical services, control dependencies and known overlaps.
Use the capability groups in this document as the initial taxonomy. Allow only deliberate changes through Enterprise Architecture.
Classify each candidate as enterprise platform, domain platform, shared service, local product, tolerated duplicate, or retiring platform.
Assign platform owner, engineering owner, business capability owner, control-function contacts, sourcing owner and financial owner.
Start with overlapping workflow, low-code, AI, integration, service management, hosting and customer-channel capabilities.
Document mandatory use cases, optional use cases, prohibited use cases, exception criteria and migration triggers.
Expose platform services, patterns, templates, documentation, controls, service levels, support and usage metrics.
Automate control evidence for security, change, access, resilience, data, privacy, AI, vendor and operational processes.
Use adoption data, risk, cost and strategic-fit evidence to consolidate, contain, migrate or retire duplicate capabilities.

maturityModel.description: A product or system is used by more than one team, but there is no formal platform mandate, service catalogue, RACI or evidence model.
maturityModel.management_implication: Do not mandate enterprise reuse. Stabilize ownership and classify capability scope.

maturityModel.description: The service has an owner, support path, basic standards and known consumers, but limited product management and self-service.
maturityModel.management_implication: Can be recommended for selected use cases. Build product obligations and control evidence.

maturityModel.description: The platform has a formal mandate, service catalogue, roadmap, onboarding, support, operational model, embedded controls and metrics.
maturityModel.management_implication: Can become the preferred platform home for defined capabilities.

maturityModel.description: The platform enables high self-service, composable APIs, automated controls, mature operations, adoption analytics, FinOps and continuous improvement.
maturityModel.management_implication: Can support mandatory reuse, strategic consolidation and advanced automation.

maturityModel.description: The platform acts as a governed control plane across products, runtime environments, data, AI, security and operations with policy-as-code and automated assurance.
maturityModel.management_implication: Use for critical enterprise capabilities where strong standardization and evidence automation create strategic value.

platformCapabilityChecklist.checklist_question: Is the platform scope formally defined by capability, consumer group, mandatory use cases and prohibited use cases?
Are platform product ownership, engineering ownership, business ownership, control ownership and contract ownership explicitly separated?
Are consumer personas, onboarding journeys, support channels and feedback loops defined?
Does the platform expose stable APIs, events, portals, templates, integration patterns or other consumption interfaces?
Are standards, golden paths, reference architectures and exception rules documented?
Are identity, secrets, privileged access, policy enforcement, vulnerability management and logging embedded?
Are data classification, lineage, retention, privacy, model/agent controls and AI governance requirements addressed where relevant?
Are SLOs, monitoring, incident/change/problem processes, runbooks and resilience requirements defined?
Can the platform produce evidence for access, change, risk, data, resilience, vendor and control obligations?
Are cost drivers, licenses, consumption metrics, showback/chargeback and optimization responsibilities visible?
Are roadmap, lifecycle state, upgrade path, decommissioning, exit and migration triggers defined?
Are adoption, value, reliability, cost, risk and consumer-satisfaction metrics reported?

platformCharterTemplate.content_to_capture: Neutral platform name and capability group.
Business capability platform, technology foundation platform, control platform, data/AI platform, enterprise service platform, workplace platform, or corporate platform.
What the platform is accountable for and what it is not accountable for.
Consumer personas, consuming teams, business domains, and critical services supported.
Reusable services, interfaces, onboarding methods and support channels.
Use cases where the platform is the preferred or mandatory platform home.
Use cases where the platform may be used but is not the enterprise default.
Capabilities that must not be implemented on the platform without explicit exception.
Platform owner, business owner, engineering owner, control owners, sourcing owner, financial owner and consumers.
Security, privacy, data, AI, operational resilience, risk, compliance and audit evidence model.
SLOs, support model, incident/change/problem process, monitoring, resilience and recovery.
Funding, consumption metrics, license model, chargeback/showback and optimization obligations.
Current maturity, target maturity, key investments, migrations, decommissioning and dependency roadmap.
Current exceptions, expiry dates, owners, risks, and migration triggers.

references: [1] CNCF / Cloud Native Platform Engineering Community, Platforms White Paper. https://cloudnativeplatforms.com/whitepapers/platforms/ Used for the platform-as-product, self-service, and embedded-governance concepts.
[2] CNCF TAG App Delivery, Platform Engineering Maturity Model. https://tag-app-delivery.cncf.io/whitepapers/platform-eng-maturity-model/ Used for the maturity and operating-model framing.
[3] Team Topologies, Platform Engineering and Fast Flow. https://teamtopologies.com/platform-engineering Used for the principle that platforms reduce cognitive load for stream-aligned teams.
[4] The Open Group, IT4IT Standard. https://www.opengroup.org/it4it Used for value-stream-based management of IT services and digital products.
[5] EIOPA, Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) overview. https://www.eiopa.europa.eu/digital-operational-resilience-act-dora_en Used for the regulated financial-services resilience context.

[6] European Banking Authority, Guidelines on ICT and security risk management (EBA/GL/2019/04). https://www.eba.europa.eu/sites/default/files/document_library/Publications/Guidelines/2020/GLs%20on%20ICT%20and%20security%20risk%20management/872936/Final%20draft%20Guidelines%20on%20ICT%20and%20security%20risk%20management.pdf Used for ICT governance, asset mapping, segregation of control functions, and ICT risk expectations.
[7] Basel Committee on Banking Supervision, Principles for operational resilience. https://www.bis.org/bcbs/publ/d516.htm Used for critical-operations and resilience considerations.
[8] NIST, Cybersecurity Framework 2.0. https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/CSWP/NIST.CSWP.29.pdf Used for Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover control alignment.
[9] NIST, Zero Trust Architecture (SP 800-207). https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/207/final Used for identity-centric, resource-centric access principles.
[10] NIST, AI Risk Management Framework. https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework Used for AI governance, mapping, measuring, and managing risk.
[11] FinOps Foundation, FinOps Framework Phases. https://www.finops.org/framework/phases/ Used for Inform, Optimize, Operate financial-management capabilities.

[12] Microsoft Learn, Power Platform Center of Excellence Starter Kit. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/guidance/coe/starter-kit Used as a vendor-neutral inspiration for low-code governance, visibility, and adoption capabilities.

[13] Amazon Web Services, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/agentcore/ Used as vendor-neutral inspiration for agentic platform runtime, gateway, memory, identity, monitoring, and guardrail capabilities.

nodes.label: G0. Platform governance and portfolio management
nodes.description: Define which capabilities deserve platform treatment, who owns them, how they are funded, how they standardize reuse, and how exceptions are controlled.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Enterprise Architecture for taxonomy and standards; enterprise governance forum for designation decisions; Enterprise Platform Owner for each approved platform.

nodes.label: Platform classification and designation
nodes.description: Evaluate whether a system is a local tool, shared service, domain platform, or enterprise platform; maintain a formal decision record.

nodes.label: Capability ownership and scope boundaries
nodes.description: Define the business or technology capability that the platform standardizes, including adjacent capabilities it must not absorb without approval.

nodes.label: Platform portfolio and target-state roadmap
nodes.description: Maintain a portfolio view of approved platforms, overlapping capabilities, lifecycle state, and strategic target-state fit.

nodes.label: Standards and exception management
nodes.description: Define mandatory standards, approved patterns, and time-bound exceptions with risk, cost, and architectural rationale.

nodes.label: Funding and consumption economics
nodes.description: Define how platform investment, run cost, chargeback, showback, and unit economics are handled.

nodes.label: Lifecycle, decommissioning and exit
nodes.description: Create entry, upgrade, migration, and exit criteria; ensure platform decommissioning is planned and funded.

nodes.label: Platform value and adoption measurement
nodes.description: Track reuse, adoption, speed, resilience, cost, satisfaction, risk reduction, and control effectiveness.

nodes.label: Regulatory traceability
nodes.description: Map platform controls, evidence, and operational dependencies to regulatory and internal-control obligations.

nodes.label: G1. Customer and digital channel platforms
nodes.description: Provide reusable capabilities for authenticated and unauthenticated customer journeys across web, mobile, advisor-assisted, branch, and contact-center channels.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Business capability owner for customer/channel outcomes; platform owner for reusable channel platform services.

nodes.label: Journey orchestration and front-end composition
nodes.description: Reusable presentation, journey, component, and orchestration patterns for digital banking experiences.

nodes.label: Digital onboarding and servicing
nodes.description: Reusable flows for onboarding, self-service, account servicing, and customer lifecycle events.

nodes.label: Customer identity, consent and access experience
nodes.description: Customer-facing identity, consent, authentication journeys, and secure authorization integration.

nodes.label: Advisor, branch and contact-center enablement
nodes.description: Shared capabilities that let staff support customers consistently across assisted channels.

nodes.label: Personalization and content integration
nodes.description: Content, offer, campaign, and personalization capabilities with data-privacy guardrails.

nodes.label: Secure messaging and notifications
nodes.description: Reusable customer communication capabilities across authenticated messages, push, email, SMS, and letters.

nodes.label: Channel telemetry and journey analytics
nodes.description: Experience telemetry, funnel analytics, and operational insight across customer-facing journeys.

nodes.label: G2. Core banking, product and transaction platforms
nodes.description: Support accounts, ledgers, payments, lending, product configuration, transaction processing, reconciliation, and financially material controls.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Business capability owner or critical service owner for the banking capability; platform owner for the underlying platform service.

nodes.label: Account, balance and ledger services
nodes.description: Systems of record and transactional services for accounts, balances, postings, and ledger integrity.

nodes.label: Product and pricing catalogue
nodes.description: Reusable configuration of banking products, terms, fees, interest, and eligibility rules.

nodes.label: Payments initiation and processing
nodes.description: Reusable services for payment initiation, routing, clearing, settlement, and payment status.

nodes.label: Credit, lending and collateral servicing
nodes.description: Reusable capabilities for loan origination, servicing, collateral, and credit lifecycle events.

nodes.label: Cards, cash management and treasury connectivity
nodes.description: Capabilities for card services, corporate cash management, liquidity, and treasury integrations.

nodes.label: Reconciliation, settlement and control
nodes.description: Financial reconciliation, exception handling, settlement controls, and evidential audit trails.

nodes.label: Transaction event and journal services
nodes.description: Reliable event, journal, and history capabilities for downstream integration and traceability.

nodes.label: G3. Process, workflow, case and rules platforms
nodes.description: Provide reusable orchestration for business processes, long-running workflows, case handling, decisions, tasks, and audit trails.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Enterprise process/workflow platform owner; business process owners are accountable for process outcomes.

nodes.label: Business process orchestration
nodes.description: Model and execute cross-system processes, including long-running, stateful, and auditable workflows.

nodes.label: Case management
nodes.description: Manage cases, tasks, roles, queues, evidence, deadlines, and operational SLAs.

nodes.label: Rules and decision services
nodes.description: Reusable rules, eligibility, routing, and decision logic where consistency and auditability are needed.

nodes.label: Task, approval and delegation management
nodes.description: Reusable human-in-the-loop approvals, assignments, escalations, and delegation patterns.

nodes.label: Document capture and e-signature integration
nodes.description: Ingest, classify, validate, sign, archive, and link documents to business processes.

nodes.label: Process mining and optimization
nodes.description: Measure process performance, bottlenecks, rework, compliance breaks, and improvement opportunities.

nodes.label: Workflow audit trail and compliance evidence
nodes.description: Generate immutable process histories, approvals, control points, and audit evidence.

nodes.label: G4. Enterprise service management and operational workflow platforms
nodes.description: Manage IT and enterprise service processes such as incidents, changes, requests, configuration, service catalogue, knowledge, and operational workflow automation.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Service management process owner for process policy; service management platform owner for platform service quality.

nodes.label: Incident, problem and change management
nodes.description: Standard processes, workflow, approvals, communication, and evidence for service disruption and change.

nodes.label: Service catalogue and request fulfilment
nodes.description: Self-service request catalogue, approvals, fulfilment routing, and service-level tracking.

nodes.label: Configuration and service mapping
nodes.description: CMDB, service models, dependencies, technical services, business services, and criticality mapping.

nodes.label: Asset, license and configuration control
nodes.description: Technology asset, software asset, license, and configuration lifecycle management.

nodes.label: Knowledge management
nodes.description: Reusable knowledge articles, runbooks, known error records, and self-service knowledge discovery.

nodes.label: Operational workflow automation
nodes.description: Controlled automation for operational tasks, remediation, approvals, and fulfilment.

nodes.label: Third-party support integration
nodes.description: Integrations to vendors and managed service partners, including SLAs, tickets, and escalation evidence.

nodes.label: G5. Developer, low-code and automation platforms
nodes.description: Accelerate safe solution delivery through self-service developer platforms, low-code tooling, templates, DevSecOps pipelines, automation, and citizen-development guardrails.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Developer platform owner for professional development platforms; low-code platform owner/CoE for low-code governance and enablement.

nodes.label: Internal developer portal and service catalogue
nodes.description: Single access point for platform services, documentation, templates, APIs, environments, and onboarding.

nodes.label: Golden paths and solution templates
nodes.description: Reusable patterns that embed architecture, security, observability, compliance, and deployment standards by default.

nodes.label: CI/CD and DevSecOps automation
nodes.description: Build, test, scan, release, deploy, rollback, and evidence generation through standard pipelines.

nodes.label: Low-code application platform
nodes.description: Governed capabilities for low-code applications, automations, and business productivity solutions.

nodes.label: Citizen developer governance
nodes.description: Makers, environments, data connectors, DLP policies, app classification, lifecycle, and support expectations.

nodes.label: Test, quality and release automation
nodes.description: Shared testing, quality gates, performance tests, accessibility checks, and release controls.

nodes.label: Robotic and desktop automation
nodes.description: Controlled automation for user-interface or back-office task automation where API automation is not viable.

nodes.label: G6. Hosting, runtime and workload platforms
nodes.description: Provide standardized and resilient places to run workloads across legacy, mainframe, private infrastructure, public cloud, containers, serverless, and managed runtime services.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Technology platform owner for the runtime; Enterprise Architecture for workload-placement principles and cross-runtime strategy.

nodes.label: Mainframe and high-integrity transaction runtime
nodes.description: Runtime, operations, capacity, and modernization pathways for critical legacy transaction workloads.

nodes.label: Private cloud and infrastructure runtime
nodes.description: Virtualization, storage, network, and enterprise hosting capabilities for regulated workloads.

nodes.label: Public cloud landing zones
nodes.description: Standard accounts, network, identity, guardrails, policy, logging, and workload onboarding for cloud usage.

nodes.label: Container, serverless and application runtime
nodes.description: Standardized container, function, runtime, service mesh, and deployment capabilities.

nodes.label: Workload placement and hosting patterns
nodes.description: Decision rules for what runs where based on criticality, latency, data gravity, cost, resilience, and compliance.

nodes.label: Environment provisioning and configuration
nodes.description: Self-service provisioning of development, test, staging, production, and sandbox environments.

nodes.label: Capacity, performance, backup and disaster recovery
nodes.description: Capacity planning, performance engineering, backup, recovery, and DR automation for platform runtimes.

nodes.label: G7. Integration, API and event platforms
nodes.description: Enable secure, discoverable, governed and observable integration across banking systems, channels, partners, data platforms, AI agents, and operational tooling.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Integration platform owner; Enterprise Architecture for integration standards and canonical patterns.

nodes.label: API management and developer portal
nodes.description: API publication, discovery, onboarding, throttling, lifecycle, subscription, and consumer analytics.

nodes.label: Integration runtime and orchestration
nodes.description: Reusable integration services, adapters, transformations, routing, and policy enforcement.

nodes.label: Event streaming and messaging
nodes.description: Event publication, subscription, schemas, replay, retention, partitioning, and operational monitoring.

nodes.label: Managed file transfer and batch integration
nodes.description: Secure, auditable file exchange and batch integration where event or API integration is not suitable.

nodes.label: Canonical schemas and contract governance
nodes.description: Data contracts, API contracts, event schemas, versioning, and backward-compatibility controls.

nodes.label: Partner and open-banking integration
nodes.description: External integration, security, consent, throttling, regulatory interface, and partner onboarding capabilities.

nodes.label: Integration observability and resilience
nodes.description: Monitoring, tracing, queuing, error handling, retry, circuit breaking, and impact analysis for integrations.

nodes.label: G8. Data, analytics and reporting platforms
nodes.description: Provide trusted data, analytics, reporting, lineage, quality, access, and regulatory-reporting capabilities for decision-making and operational control.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Data Office or Chief Data Owner for data policy and governance; data platform owner for platform service delivery.

nodes.label: Data ingestion and landing
nodes.description: Standard ingestion, validation, capture, and landing of data from operational systems and external sources.

nodes.label: Data lake, warehouse and marts
nodes.description: Curated data storage, transformation, analytics, and data product publication capabilities.

nodes.label: Metadata, lineage and catalogue
nodes.description: Business and technical metadata, ownership, lineage, classification, and discoverability.

nodes.label: Data quality and reconciliation
nodes.description: Quality rules, exceptions, reconciliation, controls, and remediation workflows.

nodes.label: Semantic layer and BI enablement
nodes.description: Common metrics, semantic models, dashboards, and governed self-service analytics.

nodes.label: Regulatory and management reporting
nodes.description: Controlled report production, traceability, attestation, and regulatory data pipelines.

nodes.label: Data access, privacy and retention controls
nodes.description: Access policies, purpose limitation, consent, retention, anonymization, and data-protection controls.

nodes.label: G9. AI, decision and agentic platforms
nodes.description: Enable responsible and reusable AI, model, decision, knowledge, and agent capabilities with guardrails, evaluation, monitoring, human oversight, and integration to enterprise tools and data.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: AI platform owner for platform service; Data/AI Governance for AI policy and risk framework; Risk/Security for control approval.

nodes.label: Foundation model access and model catalogue
nodes.description: Approved access to models, model metadata, permitted use cases, risk tiers, and consumption controls.

nodes.label: Retrieval and knowledge-base services
nodes.description: RAG patterns, embeddings, vector stores, document controls, grounding, and source traceability.

nodes.label: Prompt, tool and agent templates
nodes.description: Reusable patterns for prompts, tools, agents, evaluations, and controlled interactions with enterprise systems.

nodes.label: Agent runtime and tool gateway
nodes.description: Secure runtime, tool access, identity propagation, session isolation, memory, and scalable agent execution.

nodes.label: AI guardrails and safety controls
nodes.description: Content safety, data leakage prevention, hallucination checks, policy enforcement, and human escalation.

nodes.label: Evaluation, monitoring and model/agent lifecycle
nodes.description: Offline and online evaluation, drift, quality, cost, risk, usage monitoring, and retirement controls.

nodes.label: Human oversight and evidential audit trail
nodes.description: Human-in-the-loop controls, explanations, approvals, traceability, and model/agent audit evidence.

nodes.label: G10. Security, identity and trust platforms
nodes.description: Provide reusable identity, access, cryptography, secrets, certificate, threat, vulnerability, and policy-enforcement capabilities across platforms and products.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Security function/CISO for policy and control outcomes; security platform owner for service quality and adoption.

nodes.label: Workforce identity and access management
nodes.description: Authentication, authorization, lifecycle, federation, privileged access integration, and access reviews.

nodes.label: Customer identity and access management
nodes.description: Reusable customer authentication, consent, strong customer authentication, and delegated access patterns.

nodes.label: Privileged access and secrets management
nodes.description: Privileged access control, secrets, credentials, vaulting, rotation, break-glass, and audit.

nodes.label: Key, certificate and cryptographic services
nodes.description: HSM, key management, certificate lifecycle, encryption services, and cryptographic standards.

nodes.label: Policy-as-code and authorization services
nodes.description: Reusable policy enforcement, entitlement decisions, least privilege, and context-aware controls.

nodes.label: Threat, vulnerability and exposure management
nodes.description: Scanning, prioritization, remediation workflow, threat intelligence, and exposure reporting.

nodes.label: Security telemetry and fraud signals
nodes.description: Reusable security events, behavioral signals, fraud detection feeds, and integration with monitoring platforms.

nodes.label: G11. Observability, resilience and IT operations platforms
nodes.description: Provide operational insight, service reliability, incident response, dependency mapping, resilience testing, and runbook automation across critical technology services.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: Service reliability or operational resilience owner for service reliability process; observability platform owner for tooling and platform service.

nodes.label: Metrics, logs and traces
nodes.description: Standard telemetry ingestion, retention, search, dashboards, and correlation across systems.

nodes.label: Service-level objectives and error budgets
nodes.description: SLOs, SLIs, operational health metrics, and service reliability targets.

nodes.label: Alerting, AIOps and event correlation
nodes.description: Actionable alerting, anomaly detection, correlation, noise reduction, and operational escalation.

nodes.label: Incident response and communication
nodes.description: Incident coordination, impact assessment, communication, war rooms, and post-incident reviews.

nodes.label: Dependency and critical-service mapping
nodes.description: Map technology dependencies to business services and critical operations.

nodes.label: Disaster recovery and resilience testing
nodes.description: DR plans, recovery testing, scenario tests, failover, and resilience evidence.

nodes.label: Runbook and remediation automation
nodes.description: Automated diagnostics, remediation, rollback, and operational runbooks.

nodes.label: G12. Workplace, productivity and collaboration platforms
nodes.description: Provide secure employee productivity, collaboration, knowledge, endpoint, communication, and information-protection capabilities without letting broad licenses blur platform boundaries.

metadata.suggestedAccountability: End-user platform owner for workplace services; Enterprise Architecture for boundary decisions when productivity tools enter development, workflow, or data-platform territory.

nodes.label: Collaboration, messaging and meetings
nodes.description: Employee collaboration, conferencing, mail, chat, shared workspaces, and communication services.

nodes.label: Document, records and knowledge management
nodes.description: Document storage, co-authoring, knowledge repositories, retention, records, and discovery.

nodes.label: Endpoint and device management
nodes.description: Device lifecycle, endpoint security, patching, configuration, application deployment, and user support.

nodes.label: Enterprise search and employee portal
nodes.description: Search, knowledge access, employee portal, and self-service entry points.

nodes.label: Productivity automation
nodes.description: Automation of individual and team productivity use cases inside approved boundaries.

nodes.label: Information protection and DLP
